unsolved_parts: insufficient evidence for stabilization techniques to mitigate unstable gradients and loss oscillation in deep neural networks
next_question: What specific stabilization methods can be applied to deep neural networks to prevent unstable gradients and loss divergence, without modifying the network architecture?
search_hint: deep neural networks, stabilization techniques, batch normalization, layer normalization, weight normalization, gradient stabilization, loss stabilization, recurrent neural networks, feedforward neural networks, unstable gradients, loss divergence

evidence_summary: Batch normalization can be applied to deep neural networks to reduce internal covariate shift and improve training speed and generalization.
Batch normalization involves standardizing the activations going into each layer, enforcing their means and variances to be invariant to changes in the parameters of the underlying layers.
Layer normalization is a variant of batch normalization that computes the mean and variance used for normalization from all of the summed inputs to the neurons in a layer on a single training case.
The use of batch normalization and layer normalization can help to mitigate unstable gradients and loss oscillation in deep neural networks.
